onboarding.body.welcome: Track your AI coding usage at a glance. CodexBar lives in the system tray and shows your quota for Claude, Codex, GitHub Copilot, and more — all without leaving your editor.
onboarding.bullet.welcome.bars: One bar per provider, primary plus secondary windows.
onboarding.bullet.welcome.toasts: Smart toasts when you near a quota threshold.
onboarding.body.providers: Choose the AI services you use. You can change this later in Preferences → Providers.
onboarding.hint.providers.empty: No providers available yet. You can finish onboarding and add them later in Preferences.
onboarding.body.sign_in: Sign in to each provider so CodexBar can read your usage. The sign-in window opens in Preferences; come back here when done.
onboarding.hint.sign_in.empty: No providers selected. You can skip this step and add accounts later from Preferences.
onboarding.body.done: CodexBar will refresh in the background. Click the tray icon anytime to open the popup, or press Win+Shift+U.
onboarding.bullet.done.rerun: Re-run this wizard from Preferences → About.
onboarding.bullet.done.cadence: Quotas, status, and toasts update every minute.
onboarding.bullet.done.pin: Pin the tray icon so it always stays visible.
first_run.title: Pin CodexBar4Windows for one click access
first_run.body: CodexBar4Windows lives in the tray. To pin it, open the overflow flyout and drag the icon next to the volume icon.
